title: A street I used to hate in Bucharest
summary: How a noisy ugly street slowly became one of your favourites.
topic: A street I used to hate in Bucharest
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a place you used to dislike but came to appreciate. Explain why your feelings changed. Use 'used to', past simple, and present simple to contrast then and now.
hintStarters: When I first moved to…, I hated the street where…
It used to feel too noisy and too…
sampleText: When I first moved to Bucharest, I hated the street where my office was. It was a long, grey road full of trolleybuses, cracked pavements, and tall concrete blocks of flats from the seventies. My first impression was that the street had no soul. I used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground because there was nothing nice to look at. After a few months, something changed. I started to notice small things between the ugly buildings. There was a woman who sold cherries from a small wooden stall in summer, and an old man who played accordion on the corner near the tram line. I learned the names of two cats who slept on the windowsill of a small shoe-repair shop. Slowly, the street became familiar. I began to understand that the concrete blocks had hundreds of families inside them, with their lights, their music, and their cooking smells in the evening. Now, three years later, I do not think the street is beautiful, but I love it. When I travel and people ask where I live, I describe that ugly road with a kind of pride. The street did not change. I changed, because I stopped looking only at the surface.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I started to notice small things
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi bắt đầu để ý đến những điều nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sold cherries from a small wooden stall
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bán anh đào từ một quầy gỗ nhỏ
con phố trở nên quen thuộc

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mùi thức ăn của họ vào buổi tối

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Used to' for past states that have changed
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Used to + V' diễn tả thói quen hoặc cảm nhận trong quá khứ mà bây giờ đã khác: 'I used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground.' (giờ không còn vậy).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Used to + verb' describes a past habit or feeling that no longer holds: 'I used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground' (it's no longer true).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Contrasting then and now
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Để so sánh quá khứ và hiện tại, kết hợp 'used to' / past simple với present simple: 'I used to hate it. Now I love it.' / 'Three years later, I love the street.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: To contrast past and present, combine 'used to' / past simple with present simple: 'I used to hate it. Now I love it.' / 'Three years later, I love the street.'

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi mới chuyển đến Bucharest, tôi ghét con phố nơi văn phòng tôi nằm. Đó là một con đường dài, xám xịt đầy xe buýt điện, vỉa hè nứt nẻ, và những khối nhà bê tông cao từ thập niên bảy mươi. Ấn tượng đầu tiên của tôi là con phố không có hồn. Tôi thường đi làm với mắt nhìn xuống đất vì không có gì đẹp để ngắm. Sau vài tháng, có gì đó thay đổi. Tôi bắt đầu để ý đến những điều nhỏ giữa các tòa nhà xấu xí. Có một người phụ nữ bán anh đào từ một quầy gỗ nhỏ vào mùa hè, và một ông cụ chơi đàn accordion ở góc phố gần đường ray xe điện. Tôi đã thuộc tên hai con mèo ngủ trên bậu cửa sổ của một tiệm sửa giày nhỏ. Dần dần, con phố trở nên quen thuộc. Tôi bắt đầu hiểu rằng các khối bê tông đó có hàng trăm gia đình bên trong, với ánh đèn, âm nhạc, và mùi thức ăn của họ vào buổi tối. Bây giờ, ba năm sau, tôi không nghĩ con phố đẹp, nhưng tôi yêu nó. Khi tôi đi xa và người ta hỏi tôi sống ở đâu, tôi mô tả con đường xấu xí đó với một niềm tự hào. Con phố không thay đổi. Tôi đã thay đổi, vì tôi ngừng chỉ nhìn ở bề mặt.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'used to' sentence for a past habit.
options: I use to walk to work with my eyes on the ground.
I used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground.
I used to walked to work with my eyes on the ground.
I am used to walk to work with my eyes on the ground.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to' + base verb for a past habit no longer true.

options: I hated the street, but now I love it.
I hated the street, so now I love it.
I hated the street, because now I love it.
I hated the street, although now I love it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' is the natural contrast between past dislike and present love.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'soul' mean in 'the street had no soul'?
options: religion
personality, life, or character
a piece of music
a number
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Soul' here is a figurative word for personality or feeling.

options: the street where my office was
the street what my office was
the street which my office was
the street who my office was
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Where' refers to a place; the other relative pronouns don't fit here.

options: known to you, easy to recognise
expensive and fashionable
noisy and ugly
completely new
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Familiar' = something or someone you know well.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present-simple sentence to contrast with the past.
options: Now, three years later, I do not think the street is beautiful, but I love it.
Now, three years later, I am not thinking the street is beautiful, but I love it.
Now, three years later, I did not think the street is beautiful, but I loved it.
Now, three years later, I have not thought the street is beautiful, but I have loved it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use present simple to describe the current state.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the meaning of 'first impression'?
options: the first time you ever drive a car
the opinion you form about something the first time you see it
the first photograph you take of a place
the most beautiful part of a city
mcQuestions.explanation: 'First impression' = the immediate opinion you form on first contact.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'started to + verb'.
options: I started to notice small things between the ugly buildings.
I started to noticing small things between the ugly buildings.
I started noticing for small things between the ugly buildings.
I started notice small things between the ugly buildings.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'on the corner ___ the tram line.'
options: near
in
for
of
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Near' = close to; fits 'the corner near the tram line'.

options: The street did not change. I changed.
The street did not changed. I am changed.
The street not changes. I changes.
The street is not change. I am change.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple with 'did not' + base verb is correct.

options: to feel grateful or recognise the value of something
to ignore something completely
to repair something quickly
to fear something new

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever changed your mind about a place or a person you used to dislike? What made the change possible?
